note:
从3月份开始，当冠状病毒大流行迫使美国各地的学校关闭、企业放弃办公室、数百万人在家中隔离时，硅谷不得不改变员工的工作方式。在这些变化中，公司提供了灵活的工作时间和更多的假期，以帮助他们队伍中的照顾者和员工照顾他们的家庭。
但是，尽管这些公司说他们会支持父母在家照看孩子，但并非所有的经理和同事都同意这一点。随着时间的推移，科技行业涌现出一股针对那些工作和家庭时间分开的人的不满暗流，并在员工留言板、公司聊天软件和社交网络上公开流露出来。在Facebook，这种阻力迫使首席运营官谢丽尔·桑德伯格(Sheryl Sandberg)为公司的政策辩护。桑德伯格本人也是一名家长。
为我们的编辑订阅CNET Now时事通讯，精选当天最重要的新闻。
根据“纽约时报”的一篇报道，桑德伯格在8月份的一次会议上说，我确实认为父母有一定的挑战。但是每个人都有挑战，而这些挑战是非常、非常真实的。
与此同时，苹果(Apple)、Facebook和优步(Uber)的一些员工表示，他们几乎没有让一切都正常运转。
在接受Care.com调查的1000人中，超过一半的人表示，由于在疫情期间既要照顾孩子，又要工作，他们觉得自己让同事失望了。在8月份公布的这项调查中，52%的受访者表示，他们隐瞒了自己的育儿问题，因为他们担心同事们不会理解。45%的人认为他们的职业发展受到了影响，因为他们在家里忙于工作和孩子。
优步(Uber)首席多样性和包容性官李波(Bo Young Lee)表示，即使是那些有相对充足的托儿服务的人，他们也在苦苦挣扎。优步拥有约2.2万名员工。她补充说，在过去的几个月里，随着全球冠状病毒病例超过2600万例，导致至少86.7万人死亡，优步的管理团队越来越多地接受了一条新的口头禅。她补充说，有一段时间，人们认为这一切都会过去，一切都会恢复正常。&#34；不会恢复正常。&#34；
随着疫情的蔓延，许多科技公司扩大了政策范围，帮助父母在全职工作的同时应对照顾孩子的突如其来的责任。一些公司，如谷歌和微软，延长了带薪假期。苹果(Apple)、Facebook和优步(Uber)等公司也强调愿意允许更多可变的工作时间安排。
这些努力让科技员工能够灵活地继续工作，尽管大多数人表示，他们计划在可预见的未来让孩子留在家里，以避免潜在的病毒接触。Blind是一家面向员工的匿名社交网络应用，该公司使用员工的电子邮件地址验证人们在哪里工作。Blind在7月份的一项调查中，1,053名科技行业员工中有69%的人表示，他们的孩子将留在家里。
戴尔(Dell)、推特(Twitter)和Yelp等一些公司正在公开接受家庭承诺，在全公司范围内定期讨论这一问题，并鼓励经理和同事相互支持。
其他科技公司对照顾者员工和媒体表达了同样的观点。但一些员工表示，这些公司没有成功地将这些感受融入到他们强硬的文化中，在疫情爆发之前，这些文化往往包括人们会忍受长途通勤到办公室，这样他们就可以坐在办公桌前工作到深夜。
这在科技公司内部引发了令人惊讶的冲突，母公司员工了解到，一些经理和同龄人对父母获得的福利和灵活性感到不满。许多家长还报告说，他们需要更多的时间来完成任务，部分原因是孩子经常打断他们。招聘网站ZipRecruiter在7月份对1726名活跃求职者进行的一项调查发现，在家带着学龄儿童的母亲预计工作时间将减少9%，而父亲表示，他们预计工作时间将减少5%。
综上所述，这些新的工作安排导致一些非父母员工指责父母在没有尽到自己的责任的同时，得到了管理层更好的对待。
一名未获授权与媒体讨论内部事务的苹果员工，在要求更灵活的时间表时遇到了令人沮丧的阻力。这名员工想与一位有工作的配偶分开照顾他们蹒跚学步的孩子和十几岁之前的孩子，后者正在远程学习。一位经理回应说，这名员工应该全职工作，或者根本不工作。&#34；非正式的。&#34；
这个人的配偶说：我很高兴我还没有休任何COVID假。
这位经理没有遵循苹果的政策，苹果公司重申，我们团队中的每一位家长和照顾者都要灵活、合作和包容。对每个人来说，这都是一个艰难的时期，尤其是父母。苹果发言人克里斯汀·休格特(Kristin Huguet)在春季表示，我们希望尽我们所能支持苹果大家庭的每一名成员。
为了支持员工，苹果的人力资源团队一直在全公司范围内参加会议，鼓励员工解决他们可能遇到的任何问题。该公司还与它在精神健康服务方面的合作公司ComMental和Sanvello密切合作，帮助人们应对大流行带来的压力。首席执行官蒂姆·库克在与公司的广泛沟通中也讨论了员工及其家人面临的困境。
可以肯定的是，并不是每个家长都有这些糟糕的经历，许多人说他们的公司正在支持他们。
尽管如此，这类与流氓经理或有毒员工的经历让许多家长感到不安。
波士顿咨询集团(Boston Consulting Group)在3月和4月对美国、英国、法国、德国和意大利的3055人进行了调查，发现超过三分之一的受访者担心，与没有家庭义务的同事相比，他们的表现会不公平。
在Blind&39；7月份的调查中，硅谷父母中的这一数字跃升至62%。Facebook排名最差，83%的父母表达了这些担忧。在亚马逊，这一比例为76%。在苹果和谷歌，这一比例为65%。在微软，这一比例为60%。
公司表示，他们正试图通过让孩子成为工作生活的一部分变得更加正常，从而改变他们的文化。Yelp说，首席执行官杰里米·斯托普尔曼(Jeremy Stoppelman)刚刚为人父母，他已经在全公司的电子邮件中讨论了兼顾育儿和工作职责的问题。戴尔创始人兼首席执行官迈克尔·戴尔和Twitter首席执行官杰克·多尔西主持了在线故事时间活动，他们在白天为员工的孩子们朗读故事。谷歌和微软已正式延长带薪探亲假。优步已经在整个公司范围内分享了它的住宿政策。
想一想最近在Blind上的一次对话。Facebook的一名员工写道：我们勉强度日。在得知他或她的配偶的工作不受支持后，这位员工补充说，这给照看孩子和做家务带来了更大的压力，我自己几乎不能工作。
尽管一些人表示支持，但也有许多人予以回击。微软的一名技术员工表示：你做出了这些选择。亚马逊的另一位员工说，他妈的去他妈的男人吧。拯救你不是美国的工作，Yelp写了三分之一。
Facebook前安全主管亚历克斯·斯塔莫斯(Alex Stamos)目前在斯坦福互联网天文台(Stanford Internet Observatory)任教。他在一系列推文中表示，他怀疑这家社交网络巨头内部这样的批评言论是由年轻的男性工程师推动的。他说，他们是我听到的唯一抱怨的人。
考虑到调查数据和这些情绪，亚马逊没有回应关于它是如何努力塑造其文化的置评请求。Facebook、微软和Yelp表示，他们正在倾听员工的意见，并定期与经理合作，确保他们为员工提供支持。
美国是疫情最严重的地区之一，有600多万确诊病例，18.7万人死亡，许多父母正被迫在孩子的健康和生计之间做出选择。学校在重新开学后不久就检测到病毒在校园内传播，导致许多家长决定把孩子留在家里。但这也带来了一系列问题，特别是在硅谷全天候的紧缩文化中。
我为这个故事采访过的许多家长说，他们觉得那些没有孩子的同事，或者孩子已经长大的同事，似乎不理解他们正在经历的事情。
拥有约13.4万名员工的电脑巨头戴尔公司事务主管詹妮弗·戴维斯(Jennifer Davis)表示，员工的经历与他们的经理是谁一对一相关。在疫情爆发之前，这家计算机制造商就已经以其循序渐进的远程工作方式而闻名，此后该公司表示，预计当疫情消退时，将有超过一半的员工进行远程工作。戴尔还开始培训领导者和管理人员，让他们了解对公司文化的期望，以及如何与员工打交道。
戴尔领导层还习惯于在公司沟通中例行公事地讨论照看问题。迈克尔·戴尔(Michael Dell)在几次Zoom电话会议上给1000多名员工的孩子们读故事。高管们在他们的日程表上公开宣传家庭午餐时间，并在电话会议开始时分享心爱的人可能会闯进来。
戴尔运营总监兼副董事长杰夫·克拉克(Jeff Clarke)在8月下旬发给员工的电子邮件中写道，尽管你很有弹性，但我知道世界各地的职场父母都承受着压力，既要承担家庭义务，包括在实体和虚拟课堂上支持孩子，又要承担工作责任，这一点戴尔公司运营总监兼副董事长杰夫·克拉克(Jeff Clarke)在8月下旬写给员工的一封电子邮件中写道。虽然我们没有所有的答案，但我们重新融入我们灵活的工作文化，帮助您有更多的选择。
公司正在努力弄清楚如何确定他们应该制定什么政策，以及应该为那些可能在疫情消退时选择在家工作的人提供福利。他们说他们现在正在讨论这些想法，有些人，比如Yelp，已经宣布从明年开始提供儿童和老年人护理福利。但他们也都专注于管理短期危机。
推特(Twitter)和戴尔(Dell)表示，他们已经与公司签订了合同，例如通过在线直播课程和夏令营帮助员工的孩子。戴尔还为员工提供一对一的辅导课程和方式，让他们加入所在地区的部分付费学习舱，这可能会更好地替代工作日不堪重负的学校。
除了他的故事时间读物，迈克尔·戴尔还在他的所有员工中宣扬工作和生活的平衡。新冠肺炎明确指出了一件事--工作是你做的事情，是结果，而不是地点或时间，他在8月5日给员工的电子邮件中写道。我们希望您能够灵活地安全、高效地度过这场风暴，无论它持续多久。
他还告诉所有员工，公司将为每个人的需求制定灵活性方案。他写道，对我们每个人来说，这看起来可能都不一样，这没什么。
戴尔并不是唯一一家专注于文化的公司。这家全球最大的叫车服务公司似乎取得了一些进展。今年8月，优步制定了政策，确保照顾者可以根据需要跳过低优先级的会议，并修改整个星期的工作时间。例如，优步还告诉员工，如果他们在为孩子管理在家上学，他们可以改变工作日的班次。
李说：这让我们不仅可以对经理群体，也可以对我们的全体员工设定非常明确的期望，我们确切地认识到，对于任何负有照看责任的人来说，无论是孩子还是其他责任，情况都是多么困难。
在4月份回复Blind调查的优步护理员中，有67%的人表示，他们担心自己与同事相比会受到不公平待遇，但当我在8月份要求Blind重新进行调查时，这一比例降至51%。
李补充说，不平等一直是COVID最大的盟友。COVID做得非常好的一件事是，它暴露了任何特定社会中的每一个不平等和每一个弱点。
尊重他人，保持文明礼貌，不离题。我们删除违反我们政策的评论，我们鼓励您阅读这些评论。我们可以随时关闭讨论线程。
note_en:
Silicon Valley had to change the way its employees work when the   coronavirus pandemic forced schools to close, businesses to abandon their offices and millions of people to quarantine in their homes across the US starting in March. Among the changes,  companies offered flexible schedules and increased  time off to help caregivers and employees in their ranks take care of their families.
 But though the companies said they'd support parents at home with their children, not all managers and co-workers agreed. Over time, an undercurrent of resentment has bubbled up across the  tech industry against those splitting time between work and family, and it's spilled out in public on employee message boards, company chat software and on social networks. At Facebook, the pushback has forced COO  Sheryl Sandberg, a parent herself, to defend the company's policies.
  Subscribe to the CNET Now newsletter for our editors' picks of the most important stories of the day.
 "I do believe parents have certain challenges," Sandberg  said in an August meeting, according to a report in The New York Times. "But everyone has challenges, and those challenges are very, very real."
 Meanwhile, some employees at  Apple,  Facebook and  Uber say they're barely making it all work.
 More than half of 1,000 people surveyed by Care.com said  they felt like they'd let down their colleagues due to juggling children and work during the pandemic. Of the respondents to the survey, published in August, 52% said they hide their childcare issues because they worry colleagues won't understand. And 45% believe their career advancement has suffered because they're juggling work and kids at home.
 "Even those who have relatively adequate childcare, they're struggling," said  Bo Young Lee, chief diversity and inclusion officer at Uber, which counts about 22,000 employees in its workforce. Uber's management team has increasingly embraced a new mantra over the past few months, she added, as  coronavirus cases have topped 26 million around the world, killing at least 867,000 people. "For a while, people thought this would pass and it would all return to normal," she added. "There is no return to normal."
                                             As the pandemic spread, many tech companies expanded policies to help parents deal with the sudden responsibility of caring for children while also working full time. Some, like Google and Microsoft, extended paid time off. Companies like Apple, Facebook and Uber also emphasized willingness to allow for more-variable work schedules.
 Those efforts allow tech employees flexibility to continue working, even as a majority say they plan to keep their kids  home from school for the foreseeable future to avoid potential exposure to the virus. In a July survey by Blind, an anonymous social networking app for employees, which authenticates where people work using their employee email address, 69% of 1,053 tech industry workers said their children will stay home.
 Some companies, such as  Dell,  Twitter and  Yelp, are publicly embracing family commitments, regularly discussing the issue companywide and encouraging managers and colleagues to support one another.
  Other tech firms express the same sentiments to caregiver employees and to the press. But some employees say the companies haven't successfully woven those feelings into their hard-charging cultures, which, before the pandemic, often included the expectation that people would endure long commutes to the office so they could be at their desks, working into the evening.
 It's led to surprising clashes within tech companies, where parent employees are learning that some managers and peers resent the benefits and flexibility parents are getting. Many parents are also reporting they need more time to finish tasks, in part because of the regular interruptions caused by children. A July  survey of 1,726 active job seekers by the recruiting site ZipRecruiter found that mothers at home with school-age kids expect work hours to reduce by 9%, while fathers say they expect a drop of 5%.
 Taken together, these new working arrangements have led some nonparent employees to accuse the parents of being treated better by management while failing to pull their weight.
 One Apple employee, who isn't authorized to discuss internal matters with the press, encountered frustrating resistance when asking for a more flexible schedule. The employee wanted to split, with a working spouse, the care of their toddler and their preteen, who's learning remotely. A manager responded that the employee was expected to work full time, or not at all. "Unofficially."
 "I'm glad I didn't take any of my COVID leave already," this person's spouse said.
 The manager wasn't following Apple's policy, which the company reiterated is "to be flexible, collaborative and accommodating of every parent and caregiver on our teams." "This is a trying time for everyone -- especially parents -- and we want to do all we can to support every member of our Apple family," Apple spokeswoman Kristin Huguet said in the spring.
 In efforts to support employees, Apple's HR team has been joining meetings throughout the company, encouraging people to reach out with any issues they might have. The company's also worked closely with firms it's partnered with for mental health services, ComPsych and Sanvello, to help people cope with the stress the pandemic's caused. And CEO Tim Cook has also discussed the struggles employees and their families are facing in his broad communications with the company.
 To be sure, not every parent is having these bad experiences, and many say their companies are supporting them.
 Still, these types of experiences with rogue managers or toxic employees are unsettling many parents.
 When the Boston Consulting Group surveyed 3,055 people across the US, UK, France, Germany and Italy, in March and April, it found more than a third of respondents were worried  their performance would be unfairly compared with that of colleagues without family obligations.
 In Blind's July survey, the number jumped to 62% among Silicon Valley parents. Facebook ranked worst, with 83 perecent of parents expressing those concerns. At  Amazon, it was 76% of parents. At Apple and Google, it was 65%. At  Microsoft it was 60%.
  Companies say they're trying to shift their culture by making it more normal for children to be a part of work life. Yelp said  CEO Jeremy Stoppelman, who's a new parent, has discussed juggling childcare with work responsibilities as part of companywide emails. Dell founder and CEO  Michael Dell and Twitter CEO  Jack Dorsey have hosted online story-times where they read to their employees' children during the day. Google and Microsoft have officially extended paid family leave. And Uber has shared its accommodation policy across the company.
  Consider a recent conversation on Blind. "We are barely surviving," one employee at Facebook wrote. After sharing that his or her spouse's work wasn't supportive, putting more pressure on babysitting and chores, the employee added, "I can barely work myself."
 Though some were supportive, many others pushed back. "You made these choices," one tech employee from Microsoft said. "Man the fuck up," another at Amazon said. "It's not America's job to save you," wrote a third from Yelp.
 Alex Stamos, a former   head of security at Facebook now teaching at the Stanford Internet Observatory, said  in a series of tweets he suspects the critical comments like these inside the social networking giant were driven by young male engineers. They, he said, are the "only people I heard complain."
 Amazon didn't respond to requests for comment about how it's working to shape its culture given the survey data and these sentiments. Facebook, Microsoft and Yelp say they're listening to employees and are regularly working with managers to ensure they're supporting their staff.
   In the US -- one of the pandemic's worst hot spots, with more than 6 million confirmed cases and 187,000 people dead -- many parents are being forced to choose between their children's health and their livelihoods. Schools have been detecting the virus  spreading on their campuses shortly after they've reopened, leading many parents to decide to keep their children home. But that's come with its own set of problems, particularly in the 24/7 crunch culture of Silicon Valley.
 Many of the parents I spoke to for this story say they feel as though colleagues who haven't had children, or whose children are grown, don't seem to understand what they're going through.
  "An employee's experience is one-to-one directly correlated to who their manager is," said  Jennifer Davis, head of corporate affairs at computer giant Dell, which employs about 134,000 people. Before the pandemic, the computer maker was already known for its progressive approach to remote work, and it's since said it expects that more than half its employees will work remotely when the pandemic subsides. Dell's also begun training leaders and managers about what's expected of the company culture and how to approach employees.
 Dell's leadership has also taken to routinely discussing caregiving in company communications. Michael Dell has read stories to more than 1,000 of his employees' children over a few Zoom conference calls. Executives are publicly advertising family lunch times on their calendars, and starting conference calls by sharing that a loved one may barge in.
 "Resilient as you are, I know working parents everywhere are under stress to juggle family obligations, including supporting kids in physical and virtual classes, with work responsibilities," Jeff Clarke, Dell's operations chief and vice chairman, wrote in a late August email to employees. "While we don't have all the answers, we're leaning into our flexible work culture to help give you more options."
  Companies are trying to figure out how to determine what policies they should set and benefits they should offer for people who may choose to work from home when the pandemic fades. They say they're discussing these ideas now, and some, like Yelp, have announced child- and eldercare benefits starting next year. But they're all focused on managing the short-term crisis too.
 Twitter and Dell say they've contracted with companies to help employee's children with live classes and camps online, for example. Dell's also offering one-on-one tutoring sessions and ways for employees to join a partially paid-for "learning pod" in their area, offering a potentially better alternative to  overburdened schools during the workday.
 And in addition to his story-time readings, Michael Dell has also preached work-life balance among all his employees. "COVID-19 has made one thing clear -- work is something you do, an outcome, not a place or a time," he wrote in an Aug. 5 email to employees. "We want you to have the flexibility to weather this storm safely and productively, however long it lasts."
  He also told all employees that the company would work out flexibility for everyone's needs. "It will likely look different for each of us, and that's OK," he wrote.
 Dell isn't the only company focusing on culture. The world's largest ride-hailing service appears to have made some headway. In August, Uber codified policies ensuring that caregivers are allowed to skip low-priority meetings as needed and modify their work hours throughout the week. Uber also told employees they're allowed to shift their workday if they're managing homeschooling for their children, for example.
 "This allows us to set very clear expectations across not just our manager population but also our entire workforce that we recognize exactly how difficult the situation is for anyone with a caregiving responsibility, whether it's children or any other responsibility," Lee said.
 While 67% of Uber's caregiver employees who responded to Blind's survey in April said they're worried they're being inequitably compared with their colleagues, that number dropped to 51% when I asked Blind to rerun the survey in August.
 "Inequity has been COVID's greatest ally," Lee added. "The thing that COVID has done really well is it has exposed every inequality and every weak point in any given society."

title: 新冠肺炎期间，科技公司面临着对母公司员工日益增长的怨恨
title_en: Tech Firms Face Growing Resentment Toward Parent Employees During COVID-19

note:
上周，印度又禁止了118个与中国有链接的应用程序。绝地求生移动精简版和绝地求生移动北欧地图也包括在这一全面禁令中。现在，绝地求生公司表示，他们正在想方设法将这些应用程序带回印度。
这家韩国公司之所以被列入禁令，是因为这些手机游戏是由中国的腾讯发布的。在今天分享的一份声明中，绝地求生公司表示，它将不再使用腾讯游戏在印度发布绝地求生移动专营权。根据TechCrunch的数据，在禁令颁布之前，绝地求生移动在印度拥有4000多万月度活跃用户，因此有强烈的动机恢复这款游戏。
该公司表示：“展望未来，绝地求生公司将承担国内的所有出版责任。”随着该公司探索在不久的将来为印度提供自己的绝地求生体验的方法，它致力于通过为粉丝维持一个本地化和健康的游戏环境来做到这一点。
自6月份以来，印度已经禁止了177个与中国相关的应用程序。电子和信息技术部表示，这样做是“为了印度的主权和完整，为了印度的国防和国家安全”。它已经表达了对用户数据如何使用和存储的担忧。
美国也在考虑禁止与中国有链接的应用程序。特朗普总统下令TikTok所有者字节跳动出售其美国业务，并发布行政命令禁止腾讯的微信。这些命令极有可能在法庭上受到挑战，而且腾讯在几家美国公司都有投资，包括Snapchat的母公司Riot Games和Epic Games，这让命令变得复杂起来。它还在美国出版绝地求生移动。
note_en:
Last week, India  banned another 118 apps with links to China.  PUBG Mobile Lite and PUBG Mobile Nordic Map were included in that sweeping ban. Now, PUBG Corporation says it’s looking for ways to bring the apps back to India.
 The South Korean company was included in the ban because the mobile games are published by China’s Tencent. In  a statement shared today, PUBG Corporation said it will no longer use Tencent Games to publish the  PUBG Mobile franchise in India. According to   TechCrunch, prior to the ban,  PUBG Mobile had more than 40 million monthly active users in India, so there’s a strong incentive to restore the game.
  “Moving forward, PUBG Corporation will take on all publishing responsibilities within the country,” it said. “As the company explores ways to provide its own  PUBG experience for India in the near future, it is committed to doing so by sustaining a localized and healthy gameplay environment for its fans.”
 Since June, India has banned  177 China-linked apps. The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology says it is doing so “in the interest of sovereignty and integrity of India, defense of India and security of the State.” It has expressed concern over how user data is used and stored.
 The US is also considering banning apps with links to China. President Trump ordered TikTok owner  ByteDance to sell off its US business and issued an executive order to  ban Tencent’s WeChat. The orders will most likely be  challenged in court and complicated by the fact that Tencent has investments in several US companies, including Snapchat’s parent company, Riot Games and Epic Games. It also publishes  PUBG Mobile in the US.
title: “绝地求生手机”将与腾讯断绝关系以逃避印度禁令
title_en: 'PUBG Mobile' Will Escape India Ban By Cutting Out Tencent

(路透社)-大数据公司Sumo Logic Inc.周二表示，正寻求在美国首次公开募股(IPO)中筹资3.108亿美元，该公司的估值可能超过20.7亿美元。
该公司表示，预计将以每股17美元至21美元的价格出售1,480万股票。
大数据公司分析可以从社交媒体、网络搜索、财务记录和其他堆积如山的数字事实和数字中挑选出来的海量信息。
总部位于加利福尼亚州红木城的Sumo Logic生产基于云的工具来大规模处理数据，该公司公布亏损9210万美元，而去年同期亏损4780万美元。
相扑逻辑公司bit.ly/2RiOMId在一份文件中表示，与目前股东老虎全球管理公司(Tiger Global Management)有关联的实体已表示有兴趣以IPO价格购买至多10%的相扑逻辑公司股票。
该公司表示，上市后，风险投资公司Greylock的附属实体将拥有相扑逻辑19%的股份。
摩根士丹利、摩根大通、加拿大皇家银行资本市场(RBC Capital Markets)和杰富瑞(Jefferies LLC)是该公司此次发行的主承销商。
note_en:
(Reuters) - Big data firm Sumo Logic Inc said on Tuesday it was looking to raise $310.8 million in a U.S. initial public offering that could value the company at over $2.07 billion.
 The company said it expects to sell 14.8 million shares at a price range of $17 to $21 per share.
 Big data companies analyze vast troves of information that can be culled from social media, web searches, financial records and other mountains of digital facts and figures.
 Redwood City, California-based Sumo Logic, which makes cloud-based tools to crunch data on a massive scale posted a loss of $92.1 million compared to a loss of $47.8 million a year earlier.
  Entities affiliated with current stockholder Tiger Global Management have indicated an interest in purchasing up to 10% of Sumo Logic's shares at the IPO price, the company said  bit.ly/2RiOMId in a filing.
 After the offering, entities affiliated with venture capital firm Greylock will own 19% of Sumo Logic, the company said.
 Morgan Stanley, J.P. Morgan, RBC Capital Markets and Jefferies LLC are the lead underwriters to the firm’s offering.
title: 数据分析公司Sumo Logic希望在美国首次公开募股(IPO)中筹集3.108亿美元，价格区间为每股17美元至21美元，公司估值超过20亿美元
title_en: Data analytics company Sumo Logic is looking to raise $310.8M in its US IPO at a price range of $17 to $21 per share, valuing the company at over $2B

随着世界上越来越多的电力来自间歇性的可再生能源，人们对能够在需求低迷时期捕获和储存能源，并在需要时迅速释放能源的技术的需求越来越大。
在Gravitricity，我们正在开发一种新的存储技术，它提供了锂电池和抽水蓄能的一些最好的特性。
该技术非常适合色调网络受限的用户和运营商、配电网络和主要电力用户，在1 MW至20 MW的功率范围内运行，使现有的电网基础设施在可再生能源世界中走得更远。
如果您想了解我们的最新进展，请在这里订阅我们的公司时事通讯。
我们的专利技术基于一个简单的原理：升降重来储存和释放能量。重力系统通过多条缆索将500-5000吨的重量悬挂在深井中，每条缆索都与能够提升其所占重量份额的绞车接合。然后通过提升或降低重量来吸收或产生电能。重量由张力导丝系统(申请专利)引导，以防止其摆动和损坏轴。绞车系统可通过电气传动实现精确控制，保持井下重量稳定。
每台机组可以配置为产生1到20兆瓦的峰值功率，输出持续时间从15分钟到8小时。
关键的要求是在地面上挖一个深孔；它可以是一个重新投入使用的废弃矿井，也可以是废弃的沉井。竖井深度可以从新竖井的150米到现有矿井的1500米。
电网连接是通过现代电力电子设备实现的，以便在发电和吸收电力之间快速切换，该系统可以输送有功和无功功率，以帮助电网稳定。
虽然重量系统可以单独使用，但当竖井用作压力容器时，整个系统的储能能力可以大大增加，允许使用压缩空气储能(申请专利)。在重量系统旁边操作。这涉及到在竖井顶部和竖井衬里增加一个压力密封的“盖子”，以防止泄漏。地面提供了除竖井顶部以外的爆破阻力。绞车和发电机将被装在加压空间内，这样只需要电缆就能穿过压力容器的壁面。
最初，我们将利用现有的矿井来证明这项技术。未来的部署将能够充分利用现有矿井或专门建造的竖井，允许在任何需要储存的地方进行开发。
在2019年/20年期间，我们正在进行子系统设计，并将在2020年建造一个250kW的概念演示器。我们的目标是在2022年或2023年在英国的一个废弃矿山部署我们的第一个全尺寸原型。
我们正在与荷兰和离岸制造商豪氏威马设备公司合作，并正在开发一个工业联盟，将技术推向市场。
豪氏威马是一家全球化经营的私营公司，在为世界领先的国内外公司设计和制造重型建筑设备方面拥有丰富的经验。
豪氏威马成立于1929年，最初是一家钢铁建筑公司，现在有能力开发从概念到交付的产品。他们的产品范围可细分为四大类：重型起重设备、钻井设备、管道设备和系泊系统，从独立部件到高度工程化的集成系统各不相同。
他们的业务分布在荷兰、巴西、中国、捷克和美国的办事处以及荷兰、捷克和中国的生产工厂。
国际能源署估计，为了将全球变暖控制在2摄氏度以下，全球联网的存储容量应从2014年的140千兆瓦增加到2050年的450千兆瓦。
彭博新能源财经[2018年全球能源展望]预测，到2040年，全球在能源储存方面的支出将超过6200亿美元。今天，新能源储存技术的投资每年约为50亿美元，到2040年将增长到500亿GB。
我们的技术将在输电网上提供平衡服务，并吸引网络受限的用户和发电机、配电网以及寻求可靠、快速响应和长期储电手段的主要电力用户。
更少的展示重力技术本身就是耐用和灵活的。无论在哪里部署，它都将为该地区第一个高压输电网提供频率响应和预留等平衡服务。它还可以安装在特别需要储能的地点：受限的配电网、客户电表后面，以及该技术可以提供长期系统平衡的微电网。

Gravitricity已经与全球起重、钻井和海底专家豪氏威马合作开发了250千瓦的概念演示器。总部位于荷兰的豪氏威马设备公司(Huisman Equipment BV)是Gravitricity的完美绞车合作伙伴，是正在建设的将该技术推向市场的工业联盟的核心。他们将为计划于2019-20年推出的4兆瓦全尺寸原型以及未来的系统提供绞车驱动模块。
这笔创新资金将用于设计和建造我们的250kW概念演示机，准备在2018年底进行测试。该项目将与我们的国际绞车合作伙伴以及格拉斯哥的工业系统和控制工程师合作完成。它将确认Gravitricity的商业特征，验证我们的模拟，并提供实物概念验证演示。此外，2018年，该公司还将为计划于2019年在英国建造矿轴的全尺寸原型开发场地和工程设计。
Gravitricity目前正在筹集种子资金，以匹配赠款，并希望与潜在投资者交谈，特别是那些拥有电力系统或采矿经验的投资者。
Gravitricity Ltd已被选中参加2018年3月著名的Cleantech Innovate Investor Showcase。查理·布莱尔(Charlie Blair)将介绍250千瓦概念演示器和相关投资提案的细节。这增加了Gravitricity在2017年参加的一系列展示和推介活动，包括绿色科技挑战赛(Greentech Challest)、Clean&amp；Cool和Rushlight。
Lazards发布了他们的水平存储成本基准分析的第三版。这一次，这家金融咨询公司包括了自2015年第一版以来部署的许多新锂离子电池的资本支出和运营数据。
该报告可通过https://www.lazard.com/perspec...直接访问。。Lazards报告了各种使用案例，在这些案例中，储能项目现在是具有成本效益的，主要是仪表后的工业应用和电网支持应用，如动态或“增强型”频率响应。这两种方法都需要高功率和快速响应，并且对重力很有意义。
Gravitricity与伦敦帝国理工学院的专家合作，了解我们的存储成本如何比较。我们的快速响应、多周期技术非常适合任何需要高c速率和几个每日周期的应用。*正如预期的那样，我们的水平能源成本低于Lazard报告中针对我们目标的应用中的锂离子和任何其他技术。
评论员们借机细读了拉扎德报告的字里行间，绿色科技传媒对锂离子往返效率的观察非常值得一读。Https://www.greentechmedia.com...。
Gravitricity正在与帝国理工学院存储实验室合作，了解我们的成本与其他技术相比如何。结果非常令人鼓舞。
查理·布莱尔将于2018年2月在伦敦举行的能源储存和互联系统大会上发表讲话。
Gravitricity将于2018年2月6日在伦敦奥普林皮亚举行的REA支持的会议上发表演讲。他将在上午晚些时候的会议上发表演讲，题目恰如其分地题为“扩展到全球”(Scaling to GW；)它不仅仅是锂，还有来自液流电池、CAES和电解器技术开发商的演讲者。
查理·布莱尔(Charlie Blair)被选为英国领先的清洁技术企业家之一，他将前往硅谷，作为清洁与酷派使命的一部分。
Clean&amp；Cool是英国政府支持的英国清洁技术创新者前往旧金山的使命。Gravitricity被选为英国最令人兴奋的清洁技术创新之一，将于2017年6月10日至17日向美国创新之都的投资者和潜在合作伙伴展示。

As the world generates more and more electricity from intermittent renewable energy sources, there is a growing need for technologies which can capture and store energy during periods of low demand and release it rapidly when required.
  At Gravitricity we aredeveloping a novel storage technology which offers some of the bestcharacteristics of lithium batteries and pumped storage.
 Ideally suited tonetwork-constrained users and operators, distribution networks and major powerusers, the technology operates in the 1MW to 20 MW power range and enablesexisting grid infrastructure to go further in a renewable energy world.
 If you would like to keep up to date with our progress, please sign-up  here to our company newsletter.
  Our patented technology isbased on a simple principle: raising and lowering a heavy weight to store and release energy. The Gravitricity system suspends weights of 500 - 5000 tonnes in a deep shaft by a number of cables, each of which is engaged with a winch capable of lifting its share of the weight. Electrical power is then absorbed or generated by raising or lowering the weight. The weight is guided by a system of tensioned guide wires (patents applied for) to prevent it from swinging and damaging the shaft. The winch system can be accurately controlled through the electrical drives to keep the weight stable in the hole.
           Each unit can be configured toproduce between 1 and 20MW peak power, with output duration from 15 minutes to8 hours.
  Show less  The key requirement is a deephole in the ground; it can be a disused mineshaft brought back into use, or apurpose-sunk shaft. Shaft depths can be from 150m for new shafts down to 1500mfor existing mines.
    The grid connection is throughmodern power electronics to permit rapid switching between generation andabsorption of power and the system can deliver active as well as reactive powerto help with grid stability.
    While the weight system can beused on its own, the energy storage capacity of the overall system canbe much increased when the shaft is used as a pressure vessel, allowinga compressed air energy storage (patent applied for.) to operate alongsidethe weight system.  This involves adding a pressure-tight “lid” to the topof the shaft and lining the shaft to prevent leakage. The ground provides thebursting resistance other than at the very top of the shaft. The winches andgenerators will be contained in the pressurized space so that only electricalcables need to penetrate the pressure vessel walls.
 Initially we will prove thetechnology using existing mine shafts. Future deployments will be ableto utilise existing mines or purpose-built shafts, allowingdevelopment wherever storage is required.
        During 2019/20 we areundertaking sub-system design and will be building a 250kW concept demonstratorin 2020.  We aim to deploy our first full-scale prototype in 2022 or2023 at a disused mine in the UK.
  We are working with Dutchwinch and offshore manufacturer  Huisman Equipment BV andare developing an Industrial Consortium to take the technology to market.
        Huisman is a privately ownedcompany operating globally with extensive experience in the design andmanufacturing of heavy construction equipment for world’s leading on andoffshore companies.
  Show less  Huisman was originally foundedin 1929 as a steel construction company and now has the capacity to developproducts from concept to delivery. Their product range can be subdivided intofour main categories: Heavy Lifting Equipment, Drilling Equipment, PipelayEquipment and Mooring Systems and varies from stand-alone components to highlyengineered integrated systems.
    Their operations are dividedbetween offices in The Netherlands, Brazil, China, Czech Republic and USA andproduction facilities in The Netherlands, Czech Republic and China.
  The International Energy Agency estimates that in order to limit global warming to below 2°C, the capacity of storage connected to the grid worldwide should increase from 140 GW in 2014 to 450 GW by 2050.
  Bloomberg New Energy Finance [Global Energy Outlook 2018] has forecast over $620 billion of global spend on energy storage to 2040. Today, new energy storage technology investment is around $5 billion per year, this will grow to £50 billion by 2040.
  Our technology will provide balancing services on transmission grids as well as appealing to network-constrained users and generators, distribution networks and major power users seeking a reliable, fast response, and long-term means of storing power.
  Show less  The Gravitricity technology is inherently durable and flexible. Where ever it is deployed it will provide balancing services such as Frequency Response and Reserve to the  high voltage transmission grid in the region.  It can also be sited at locations where energy storage is particularly required: on constrained distribution grids, behind customer meters, and on micro-grids where the technology can provide long-term system balancing.
  On existing grids Gravitricity installations can be considered as long term infrastructure assets that maximise the utility of existing grid hardware.  In many situations they can be deployed to minimise or remove altogether the requirement for expensive distribution grid upgrades as demand and supply patterns change.
  On emerging grids, Gravitricity systems will significantly reduce the requirement for redundancy in grid hardware. There is very significant opportunity to reduce the cost of rural electrification across the developing world by deploying long-life storage technologies.
  Martin Wright, Chairman,  has a background in entrepreneurial company development (eg. MCT) Venture Capital, oil and gas and, earlier, as a Seaman Officer in the Royal Navy. Today he is Chairman of both the  UK Renewable Energy Association (the main UK trade association for renewable energy) and of  Mojo Maritime Ltd, a leading marine installation and operations company focused on marine Renewable Energy.
  Peter Fraenkel, Technical Director and inventor, is a chartered mechanical engineer with a background in international energy consultancy and technology development R&D, and he is also a visiting professor at the University of Edinburgh.  He was the inventor and Technical Director of  Marine Current Turbines, which deployed the world's most successful tidal turbine in Northern Ireland. In 2013 Peter received an MBE for services to Marine Energy.
  Charlie Blair, Managing Director,  joined Gravitricity in 2015 having been Head of Marine Energy in the Carbon Trust's Innovation Team. He is an experienced commercial leader, delivering millions of pounds of innovation funding, and has 15 years’ experience in clean energy technology innovation. He has supported numerous early-stage technology start-ups ranging from electric vehicles through to biomass heating. Charlie is a fellow of the Energy Institute and member of the Institution of Engineering and Technology.
  Niall Moffat, Director of Finance, is a Chartered Accountant and joined the Gravitricity team in April 2020. Having spent 10 years in practise, including 4 years with Ernst & Young in Bermuda, Niall has spent the last 15 years in both commercial and strategic roles within industry.
  Ruth Apps, Business Development Manager, joined Gravitricity in 2020. Ruth has 6 years of commercial experience, having worked as a consultant, business development for an innovation centre and business analyst. Ruth is responsible for the strategic management of business development activities for Gravitricity. She has a degree in Geology and an MSc in Environmental Technology (Energy Policy), both from Imperial College London.
  Miles Franklin, Lead Engineer, is a chartered mechanical engineer with 7 years experience in new technology innovation from a number of fields, including novel borehole drilling machines in West Africa and new product development at Dyson.  Miles leads the engineering team in Edinburgh, which also works with partner engineers at Huisman, the Edinburgh University Institute for Energy Systems, and various external contractors.
  Richard Montague, Chief Technical Advisor, is a Chartered Mechanical Engineer and joined Gravitricity part time in December 2018. He has worked for most of his career in the marine renewables industry, developing devices for both wave and tidal power companies. He has played a leading role in taking some of that sector’s highest-profile projects from early concepts through to product delivery.
  Chris Yendell, Project Development Manager, is a mechanical engineer with 9 years of experience in renewable energy project development. Having worked both on the consultant and the developer side, he has successfully delivered numerous consented onshore wind, solar and hydro power projects to the point of financial close.  Chris is currently responsible for the strategic management of project development activities for Gravitricity’s demonstration and full scale sites.
  Steven Kirk, Senior Mechanical Engineer, is a CharteredMechanical Engineer with over 9 years of experience designing and commissioningmechanical systems and structures. Steven has delivered to market a variety ofleading edge mechanical systems in the offshore, space and constructionindustries and is currently responsible for design work on Gravitricity's250kW concept demonstrator.
  Julie Le Négaret, Mechanical engineer, joined Gravitricity in 2020. She is a graduate in Mechanical Engineering (MEng) and specialised in integrated design at the University of Technology of Compiègne (France). Julie worked previously within design & manufacturing teams mainly in the aerospace industry. She is currently working on the design of the Gravitricity's 250kW concept demonstrator.
  Gavin Edwards, Senior Mechanical Engineer, has over eight years of experience specialising in the design, analysis and management of large-scale bespoke offshore handling/lifting projects, from concept through to commissioning. Gavin is currently responsible for design work on Gravitricity’s 250kW concept demonstrator. Gavin joined Gravitricity in Spring 2020.
  We are growing our engineering and commercial teams as we prepare to build our 250kW Concept Demo and 4MW full scale prototype.
 We are actively trying to build a dynamic, diverse team so we'd like to hear from anyone who's passionate about what we're doing.  If you are interested in any of the following role(s) please email as below, with a covering letter explaining why you are keen to join us.  All jobs are in Edinburgh and are full-time roles (though any employees who want to are encouraged to work a 4-day week.)
  Company Reg. No: 7827384 England  Registered office: Regent House, 316 Beulah Hill, London, SE19 3HF
 If you would like to stay up to date with Gravitricity technology developments, please  subscribe to our newsletter. We expect to send updates when there is news, and roughly every 2-3 months.  Our privacy policy is available  here should you wish to view it.
  This morning we released a press release that our demonstrator project is officially underway. Early pick-ups include, ReNews and The Times.
        Gravitricity is very pleased to have raised an additional tranche of Equity in a ‘Seed Plus’ round which completed at the end of June.  Thank you to all our new shareholders, whether you have invested £10.20 or £75,000.
    Gravitricity has signed a land rental agreement with Forth Ports to build their first demonstrator project on land within the Port of Leith.
 Work will begin on the £1 million project in October, on an industrial site at the Port of Leith with plans to be up and running by late December.
 The 16-metre high rig will utilise the port’s extensive electrical network and grid connections and will be used to demonstrate the speed of response of their innovative energy storage system.
 Gravitricity’s energy battery works by raising multiple heavy weights – totalling up to 12,000 tonnes – in a deep shaft and releasing them when energy is required. We plan to roll out our technology in disused mine shafts worldwide.
 The demonstrator at the Port of Leith will allow the technology to be trialled on a much smaller scale, utilising an above ground structure.
  “This grid-connected demonstrator will use two 25-tonnes weights suspended by steel cables. In our first test we’ll drop the weights together to generate full power and verify our speed of response. We calculate we can go from zero to full power in less than a second – which can be extremely valuable in the frequency response and back-up power markets
 “We will then run tests with the two single weights, dropping one after the other to verify smooth energy output over a longer period. Together, this two-month test programme will confirm our modelling and give us valuable data for our first full-scale 4MW project which will commence in 2021.”
    Gravitricity has won a grant under InnovateUK's Energy Catalyst programme, which is supporting UK businesses when developing new technologies for energy access in Sub-Saharan Africa and Asia.
 The £300,000 grant, means Gravitricity will undertake site and project feasibility work in Southern Africa, with project partners, RESA Energy.
   We ran a crowdfunding campaign in October 2019 and received a very positive response. The campaign reached its initial target of £246,000 on the first day of an intended 30 day campaign. The raise ultimately attracted investment of over £750,000 before hitting its upper limit and closing to further interest, all in the first week. Gravitricity are truly grateful for the support and are now all set to commence fabrication of our proof of concept demonstrator.
 We proudly share below our crowdfunding video, filmed with some of our team at our offices in Edinburgh and the National Mining Museum Scotland by local filmmakers Melt the Fly. Please note the investment round is now closed.
    Charlie and Miles spent a week in the Netherlands at the beginning of October after Gravitricity were selected as one of 7 start ups out of 300 entrants in the New Energy Challenge. The competition, which is jointly organised by Shell, Rockstart, YES!Delft and Get In The Ring, offered finalists the chance to exchange ideas with coaches and experts as well as validate business plans.
    Charlie is speaking on energy access and green finance at the event in the Hague.
 Here is Charlie giving an elevator pitch on Gravitricity at the event. Click the image to see the video.
           Imperial College Analsysis suggests early Gravitricity projects will be cheaper than lithium for frequency response applications.
 A report by independent analysts at Imperial College London predicts that Gravitricity’s gravity-fed energy storage system may offer a better long-term cost of energy storage than batteries or other alternatives – particularly in grid balancing and rapid frequency response services.
 Gravitricity's first-of-a-kind 4MW prototype system will be cheaper than batteries on a levelised cost of storage basis.  The modelling emphasizes the importance of long life and lack of degradation for revenue stacking.
  News links at  Business Green and  Engineer News Network.  Please contact Gravitricity by email if you would like a copy of the report.
   Gravitricity shortlisted for UK's Renewable Energy Association  Innovation award and Lead Engineer Miles Franklin nominated for Scottish Renwables young Professionals award
      Gravitricity teams up with worldwide lifting, drilling and subsea specialists Huisman to build prototype energy store
  Gravitricity has teamed up with worldwide lifting, drilling and subsea specialists Huisman to develop the 250kW  Concept Demonstrator. Dutch-based Huisman Equipment BV are a perfect winch partner for Gravitricity and form the centre-piece of the Industrail Consortium being built to take the technology to market.  They will supply winch-drive modules for the 4MW full-scale prototype planned for 2019-20, and future systems.
    This innovation funding will be used to design and build our 250kW concept demonstrator, ready for testing in late 2018.  The project will be completed in collaboration with our international winch partner and with controls engineers Industrial Systems & Controls in Glasgow.  It will confirm Gravitricity's commercial characteristics, validate our simulations, and provide a physical proof-of-concept demonstration.     During 2018 the company will also develop the site and engineering designs for the full-scale prototype, planned for a UK mineshaft in 2019-20.
 Gravitricity is now raising seed funding to match the grant and would like to speak to potential investors, particularly those with power systems or mining experience.
        Gravitricity ltd has been selected for the prestigious Cleantech Innovate Investor Showcase in March 2018.  Charlie Blair will be presenting details of the 250kW Concept Demonstrator and the associated investment proposition. This adds to a list of showcasing and pitching events that Gravitricity has attended in 2017, including the Greentech Challenge, Clean & Cool, and Rushlight.
     Lazards have published Version 3 of their Levelised Cost of Storage benchmarking analysis.  This time the financial advisory firm have included capex and operating data from numerous new Lithium Ion batteries deployed since Version 1 in 2015.
 The report can be accessed directly at  https://www.lazard.com/perspec... . Lazards report various use-cases where Energy Storage projects are now cost effective, primarily behind-the-meter industrial applications and grid-support applications such as Dynamic or ‘Enhanced’ Frequency Response. Both of these require high-power and rapid response and are of interest to Gravitricity.
 Gravitricity has worked with experts from Imperial College London to understand how our storage costs compare.  Our fast-response, multi-cycle technology is exceptionally well suited for any application requiring a high c-rate and several daily cycles.  As expected, our levelized cost of energy is lower than lithium ion and any other technology in the Lazard’s report for the applications we are targeting.
 Commentators have taken the opportunity to read between the lines of Lazard’s report.  Greentech Media’s observations on lithium-ion round trip efficiency are well worth a read.  https://www.greentechmedia.com...
 Gravitricity is working with Imperial College Storage Labs to understand how our costs compare to other technologies. Results are  very encouraging.
   Charlie Blair will be speaking at Energy Storage & Connected Systems in London, February 2018
 Gravitricity will be presenting at the REA supported conference at London's Oplympia on 6th February 2018. He'll be speaking in the late morning session fittingly titled 'Scaling to GW; It's not just Lithium' along with speakers from flow-battery, CAES and electrolyser technology developers.
    Charlie Blair has been selected as one of Britain's leading cleantech entrepreneurs and will be traveling to Silicon Valley as part of the Clean&Cool mission
 Clean & Cool is a UK Government supported mission for British CleanTech innovators to San Fransisco.  Gravitricity has been selected as one of the most exciting clean technology innovations coming out of the UK and will be showcased to investors and potential partners in America's capital of innovation from 10th - 17th June 2017.

note: 我在DOS上使用WordPerfect 6.2，不是出于任何怀旧或遗留原因，只是因为它是一个功能齐全、高度可配置的字处理器，我可以在终端上使用它。我只用它写信之类的，没什么大不了的，但如果可以的话，我更喜欢呆在航站楼里。它在dosemu2下工作得很好，它有一个终端模式，可以将各种VGA模式转换成S-lang调用(S-lang就像ncurses，所以不需要X11)。我发现这在技术上令人印象深刻，并使许多旧的DOS软件与本机Linux软件没有什么区别；stdin/stdout、参数、主机文件系统访问等都是透明工作的。这是一张屏幕截图：https://twitter.com/taviso/status/1272670107043368960/photo/...。它可以导入TTF字体并打印到PostScript，我只需将其导入ps2pdf，然后在主机上进行处理。我不知道还有其他功能齐全的现代文字处理器可以在xterm上运行。我知道文字研磨机，但它是非常非常基础的。您可以使用文本编辑器，但它不适合布局，因为它不理解比例字体几何形状之类的东西-您需要了解打印时线条/字形如何适应物理页面。你可以用某种形式的标记、html、tex、markdown等等来编写它，但是如果我只是想格式化一个文档，我更喜欢文字处理程序。
note_en: I use WordPerfect 6.2 for DOS, not for any nostalgia or legacy reasons, just because it's a full-featured and highly configurable word processor that I can use in a terminal. I only use it for writing letters and so on, nothing too serious, but I prefer to stay in the terminal if I can. It works beautifully under dosemu2, which has a terminal mode that can translate various VGA modes into S-Lang calls (S-Lang is like ncurses, so no X11 required). I find this technically impressive and makes a lot of old DOS software indistinguishable from native linux software; stdin/stdout, parameters, host filesystem access, etc all work transparently. Here's a screenshot:  https://twitter.com/taviso/status/1272670107043368960/photo/... It can import TTF fonts and print to PostScript, which I just pipe into ps2pdf and then handle on the host. I'm not aware of any other full-featured modern word processor that can run in an xterm. I know about wordgrinder but it's very very basic. You could use a text editor, but it's not ideal for layout because it doesn't understand things like proportional font geometries - you need that to know how lines/glyphs will fit on the physical page when it's printed. You could write it in some form of markup, html, TeX, markdown, whatever, but if I'm just trying to format a document I prefer a word processor.
title: “我仍在使用用于DOS的WordPerfect 6.2”

哟！✌️说我是布莱特！我是一名创始人，也是前认知科学研究员。《社会研究》是一份半周一期的时事通讯，面向为人类打造伟大产品的人们。它包括每周在科技推特上发生的事情的回顾，以及使用心理学、经济学和其他社会科学的框架进行的深入分析。
🚨帮我把社会科做得更好！请填写这份快速调查表格，有机会赢得25美元的亚马逊礼品卡！
🌴我们为初创企业开办了一个炒作之家--我刚刚和19位创业者一起搬进了图卢姆的一座豪宅。它就像TikTok的协作室，但可能更令人畏缩。
🏠什么是合作之家？-合作之家是现代真人秀节目。他们专注于艺人的社交媒体增长，但也将包括专业人士。
🤪首席Meme官的崛起-这些天越来越多的风投公司在大便，创造恶毒的Tik Tok视频和Meme。为什么会发生这种事？
📈品牌是职业生涯中最有价值的资产-随着时间的推移，品牌的价值会增加(技能会减少)，并可以提升任何产品的价值。
🏢微型创业者的崛起-整个企业(包括个人和风险投资规模)都建立在品牌(社交媒体追随者)的支持上。
💩是垃圾帖子适合的地方-模因和视频是推动增长的简单方式。在竞争激烈的行业(如风投)，他们习惯于嘲弄竞争，赢得交易。重要的是，他们的内容与娱乐影响者相似。
🚀将出现更专业的合作机构-由于来自专业人员的内容与来自艺人的内容更加相似，创作方法将会趋同。也就是说，更多的专业人士将选择参与合作之家。
周五，我搬进了Launch House，这是图勒姆的一座豪宅，9月份容纳了19名企业家。
就在几天前，Launch House还没有名字，更不用说网站和活跃的Instagram、Twitter和TikTok账户了。这样做的目的很简单，就是让一些人一起在一个好地方隔离，并在一个月的时间里推出一些很酷的项目。在这场流行病中，我们都感到孤立和徒劳，这就是我们的解决方案。
但后来，我们的厚颜无耻的想法占了上风，它变成了✨a Thing✨。
合作之家是由一群有影响力的人(通常在TikTok上)围绕一个主题-增长-出租的实体豪宅(通常在洛杉矶)。
如果说社交媒体正在取代电视，那么合作之家正在取代像“老大哥”和“露台屋”这样的真人秀节目。
🤝协作-通过共同创建内容来交叉授粉受众是任何创作者增长受众的主要方式之一。Collab House使协作变得轻松10倍。
📚技能共享-与同行分享所学知识是提高任何技能的最佳途径。合作公寓让这一切变得很容易。
❤️同志情谊-任何职业都有考验和磨难。合作之家为人们创造了讨论和克服它们的空间。
事实是，多年来一直有跨行业的共同生活经历，实现了其中的许多事情：
企业培训-德勤大学在德克萨斯州一家漂亮的设施为德勤员工提供培训计划。
𝘾𝙤𝙡𝙡𝙖𝙗𝙝𝙤𝙪𝙨𝙚𝙨𝙖𝙧𝙚𝙡𝙖𝙨𝙚𝙧𝙛𝙤𝙘𝙪𝙨𝙚𝙙𝙤𝙣𝙨𝙤𝙘𝙞𝙖𝙡𝙢𝙚𝙙𝙞𝙖𝙜𝙧𝙤𝙬𝙩𝙝.。
因此，在社交媒体上建立业务的艺人(例如TikTok有影响力的人)当然会希望参与合作之家。
风险投资行业发生了一些奇怪的事情。最新一代的投资者创造表情包、垃圾帖子和Tik Tok视频，作为他们工作的核心部分。
𝙒𝙝𝙮𝙖𝙧𝙚𝙨𝙚𝙧𝙞𝙤𝙪𝙨𝙥𝙧𝙤𝙛𝙚𝙨𝙨𝙞𝙤𝙣𝙖𝙡𝙨𝙗𝙚𝙘𝙤𝙢𝙞𝙣𝙜𝙨𝙝𝙞𝙩𝙥𝙤𝙨𝙩𝙚𝙧𝙨？
在达到一定程度后，从手袋到董事会座椅，一切的价值都归结为品牌。
对于专业人士来说，更强大的品牌意味着更高的薪酬咨询工作，更好的销售/投资/招聘交易流程等。
品牌也是任何职业中最具弹性的资产之一。随着时间的推移，品牌实际上可以增加价值。另一方面，技能的价值大多在下降-随着时间的推移，技术使大多数技能变得无关紧要。
因此，专业人士不断地寻找方法来改善他们的品牌。一些例子：
许多人意识到，在Twitter这样的平台上建立受众是提升品牌的最可靠途径之一。在Twitter上，品牌也是高度可衡量的，这是以前不可能的。
投资者李瑾表示，我们正在见证工作分拆的开始，越来越多的个人选择建立独立的个人企业，而不是为公司工作。
大卫·佩雷尔(David Perell)就是一个典型的例子。大卫没有为传统公司工作，而是通过销售在线课程和天使投资(其中大部分)赚钱。推特是他的顶端。
换句话说，推特让大卫赢得了一批观众。他可以销售他喜欢的任何数量的产品来赚钱。
在这个所谓的激情经济中，其他的例子还有莱尼·拉奇茨基(时事通讯)、蒂亚戈·福特(课程)和李瑾本人(投资)。
社交媒体的追随者也被用来发展风险规模的业务。Austen Allred(LambdaSchool)、Domm Holland(Fast)、Erik Torenberg(在Deck/Village Global上)和其他人可以将他们的大部分关键员工和客户追溯到Twitter，每个人都有很强的影响力。
那么，如果在社交媒体上建立强大的追随者对于建立一家成功的企业至关重要，那么你如何做到这一点呢？
有一些行之有效的方法可以在Twitter等平台上建立受众。
一般来说，照片和视频获得的参与度要比文字内容高得多，但Tech Twitter上的大部分照片和视频都是表情包、非严肃的Tik Tok视频和其他垃圾帖子。
由于竞争如此激烈，产品之间的差异化如此之小，更多的风投正在利用嘲弄(通过垃圾帖子)作为脱颖而出和关闭竞争的一种方式。
心理学家杰恩·雷斯伯勒(Jayne Raisborough)和马特·亚当斯(Matt Adams)建议人类使用嘲弄来表示道德优越感，并为群体行为设定规范标准。通过制作嘲弄风险投资的表情包和Tik Tok视频，风投们在声明：
“那些创造严肃内容(尤其是需要花费大量精力的内容)的风投们太努力了，因此质量很低”(参见Sprezzatura)。
𝘾𝙤𝙣𝙩𝙚𝙣𝙩𝙘𝙧𝙚𝙖𝙩𝙚𝙙𝙗𝙮𝙨𝙚𝙧𝙞𝙤𝙪𝙨𝙥𝙧𝙤𝙛𝙚𝙨𝙨𝙞𝙤𝙣𝙖𝙡𝙨𝙞𝙨𝙧𝙖𝙥𝙞𝙙𝙡𝙮𝙖𝙥𝙥𝙧𝙤𝙖𝙘𝙝𝙞𝙣𝙜𝙩𝙝𝙚𝙨𝙩𝙮𝙡𝙚𝙤𝙛𝙘𝙤𝙣𝙩𝙚𝙣𝙩𝙘𝙧𝙚𝙖𝙩𝙚𝙙𝙗𝙮𝙚𝙣𝙩𝙚𝙧𝙩𝙖𝙞𝙣𝙚𝙧𝙨
因此，创建内容的方法(并因此在社交媒体上增长)也将趋同。
随着时间的推移，职业界逐渐接近非职业界(只是晚了几年)。就像T恤和牛仔裤在工作场所被接受一样，专注于社交媒体的合作场所对一些专业人士来说也会变得司空见惯。
像Type House这样的数字社区(我和本文提到的李瑾、大卫·佩雷尔和特纳·诺瓦克都是其中的一员)最近一直在爆炸式增长。这些活动在外观和行为上都与合作实验室一模一样，但对社交媒体增长的重视程度较低，显然只发生在在线…上。就目前而言。
随着远程工作继续将个人推向像Type House这样的在线社区，我相信我们将看到许多这样的社区建立像Collab House这样的IRL体验。
像Y Combinator和On Deck这样的以社区为中心的公司很可能是第一个这样做的，但如果像Google或Facebook这样的公司在某个时候也这样做，我也不会感到惊讶。
最终，这一切可能会把我们带到像巴拉吉这样的人多年来一直在谈论的问题上--主权“云城市”的出现。
地狱…。Launch House上线还不到一周，我们已经在这个地区拿下了3套不同的房子。这里成为“发射之城”只是个时间问题！
再次感谢您的阅读！请留言或回复-我很想听听您的想法！
note_en:
Yo! ✌️  I’m  Brett! I am a founder and former Cognitive Science researcher.  Social Studies is a semi-weekly newsletter for people building great products for humans. It includes recaps of what happened on Tech Twitter every week plus deep analysis using frameworks from Psychology, Economics, and the other Social Sciences.
 🚨 Help me make Social Studies even better! Please fill out  this quick survey for a chance to win a $25 Amazon gift card!
    🌴 We started a Hype House for startups - I just moved into a mansion in Tulum with 19 entrepreneurs. It’s like a TikTok collab house, but probably more cringe.
 🏠 What is a collab house? - Collab houses are modern reality TV shows. They’re focused on social media growth for entertainers but will include professionals as well.
 🤪 The rise of the Chief Meme Officer - More and more VCs are shitposting these days, creating satyrical tik tok videos and memes. Why is this happening?
 📈 Brand is the most valuable asset for a career - Brand increases in value over time (skills decrease) and can drive up the value of any product.
 🏢 The rise of micro-entrepreneurs - Entire businesses (both solopreneurs and venture scale) are being built on the backs of brands (social media followings).
 💩Where shitposts fit in - Memes and videos are easy ways to drive growth. In competitive industries (like VC) they are used to mock competition and win deals. Importantly, they resemble content similar to entertainment influencers.
 🚀 More professional collab houses will emerge - As content from professionals more closely resembles content from entertainers, methods for creation will converge. Namely, more professionals will choose to participate in collab houses.
    On Friday, I moved into Launch House, a mansion in Tulum housing 19 entrepreneurs for the month of September.
    Just a few days ago, Launch House didn’t have a name, let alone a  website and active  Instagram,  Twitter, and TikTok accounts. The point was simply to get some people to quarantine together in a nice place and launch some cool projects over the course of a month. We had all been feeling isolated and unproductive amidst the pandemic, and this was our solution.
 But then our cheeky ideas got the best of us and it became ✨ a thing✨
   Collab houses are physical mansions (usually in LA) rented out by groups of influencers (usually on TikTok) centered around one theme - growth.
 If social media is replacing television, collab houses are replacing reality TV shows like Big Brother and Terrace House.
   🤝 Collaboration - Cross pollinating audiences by creating content together is one of the main ways any creator grows their audience. Collab houses make collaborating 10x easier.
 📚 Skill sharing - Improving at any craft is best done by sharing learnings with peers. Collab houses make this easy.
 ❤️ Camaraderie - There are trials and tribulations with any career. Collab houses create spaces for people to discuss and overcome them together.
 The truth is, there there have been co-living experiences across industries that accomplish many of these things for years:
 Corporate training -  Deloitte University offers training programs to Deloitte employees at a beautiful facility in Texas.
  𝘾𝙤𝙡𝙡𝙖𝙗 𝙝𝙤𝙪𝙨𝙚𝙨 𝙖𝙧𝙚 𝙡𝙖𝙨𝙚𝙧 𝙛𝙤𝙘𝙪𝙨𝙚𝙙 𝙤𝙣 𝙨𝙤𝙘𝙞𝙖𝙡 𝙢𝙚𝙙𝙞𝙖 𝙜𝙧𝙤𝙬𝙩𝙝.
 So, of course entertainers who build their businesses on social media (e.g. TikTok influencers) would want to participate in collab houses.
    There’s something strange happening in the venture capital industry. The newest generation of investors create memes, shitposts, and tik tok videos as a core part of their job.
            𝙒𝙝𝙮 𝙖𝙧𝙚 𝙨𝙚𝙧𝙞𝙤𝙪𝙨 𝙥𝙧𝙤𝙛𝙚𝙨𝙨𝙞𝙤𝙣𝙖𝙡𝙨 𝙗𝙚𝙘𝙤𝙢𝙞𝙣𝙜 𝙨𝙝𝙞𝙩𝙥𝙤𝙨𝙩𝙚𝙧𝙨?
   After a certain point, the value everything - from handbags to board seats - comes down to brand.
   For professionals, a stronger brand means higher paying consulting gigs, better deal flow for sales/investing/hiring, etc.
 Brand is also one of the most resilient assets to any career. Brands can actually increase in value over time. Skills, on the other hand, mostly decrease in value - technology makes most skills irrelevant over time.
 As a result, professionals have constantly sought out ways to improve their brands. Some examples:
  What many are realizing is that building an audience on a platform like Twitter is one of the most reliable path towards increasing one’s brand. On Twitter, brand is also highly measurable in a way that was not previously possible.
   Investor Li Jin says we’re witnessing the beginnings of the unbundling of work, where more and more individuals are choosing to build independent single-person businesses instead of working for companies.
  David Perell is a classic example. Instead of working for a traditional company, David makes his money (a lot of it) selling online courses and angel investing. Twitter is his top of funnel.
 Put another way, Twitter has allowed David to capture an audience. He can sell any number of products he likes to monetize it.
  In this so-called passion economy, other examples include Lenny Rachitsky (newsletters), Tiago Forte (courses), and Li Jin herself (investing).
 Social media followings are also being utilized to grow venture-scale businesses as well. Austen Allred (LambdaSchool), Domm Holland (Fast), Erik Torenberg (On Deck/Village Global) and others can trace the majority of their key hires and customers back to Twitter where each has a strong presence.
   So if building a strong following on social media is crucial to building a successful business, how do you do that?
  There are some tried and true methods for building an audience on platforms like Twitter.
  In general photos and videos receive much more engagement than text content but most of the photo and videos on Tech Twitter are memes, non-serious Tik Tok videos and other shitposts.
   Since there’s so much competition and so little differentiation between products, more VCs are utilizing mockery (by way of shitposts) as a way to stand out and shut down competition.
 Psychologists Jayne Raisborough and Matt Adams suggested that humans use mockery to signal moral superiority and set normative standards for behavior in groups. By creating memes and Tik Tok videos mocking venture capital, VCs are stating:
 “VCs who create serious content (especially content that takes a lot of effort) are trying too hard, and are thus low quality” (see  Sprezzatura)
   𝘾𝙤𝙣𝙩𝙚𝙣𝙩 𝙘𝙧𝙚𝙖𝙩𝙚𝙙 𝙗𝙮 𝙨𝙚𝙧𝙞𝙤𝙪𝙨 𝙥𝙧𝙤𝙛𝙚𝙨𝙨𝙞𝙤𝙣𝙖𝙡𝙨 𝙞𝙨 𝙧𝙖𝙥𝙞𝙙𝙡𝙮 𝙖𝙥𝙥𝙧𝙤𝙖𝙘𝙝𝙞𝙣𝙜 𝙩𝙝𝙚 𝙨𝙩𝙮𝙡𝙚 𝙤𝙛 𝙘𝙤𝙣𝙩𝙚𝙣𝙩 𝙘𝙧𝙚𝙖𝙩𝙚𝙙 𝙗𝙮 𝙚𝙣𝙩𝙚𝙧𝙩𝙖𝙞𝙣𝙚𝙧𝙨
 As a result, the methods for creating content (and thus growing on social media) will also converge.
   The professional world approaches with the non-professional world over time (it’s just a few years late). Just like T-shirts and jeans became accepted in the workplace, social media-focused collab houses will become commonplace for some professionals as well.
  Digital communities like the Type House (which I am a part of along with Li Jin, David Perell, and Turner Novak who were referenced in this post) have been exploding lately. These look and act exactly like collab houses but with less emphasis on social media growth and obviously only take place online… for now.
  As remote work continues to drive individuals towards online communities like Type House, I believe we’ll see many of these communities establish IRL experiences like collab houses.
 Community-centered businesses like Y Combinator and On Deck will likely be the first to do this, but I wouldn’t be surprised if companies like Google or Facebook also did at some point.
 Eventually, this may all bring us to what people like Balaji have been talking about for years - the emergence of sovereign, “cloud cities.”
  Hell… Less than 1 week into Launch House and we’ve already taken over 3 different houses in the area. It’s only a matter of time before this becomes Launch City!
   Thanks again for reading! Leave a comment or reply - would love to hear your thoughts!
